title: String Interpolation 💬
description: Mix variables gracefully into your text like magic.

slides.title: Text + Variables 🤝
slides.content:
# What is String Interpolation?

Often, we want to inject our variables dynamically into our text strings. Swift makes this incredibly easy with a feature called **String Interpolation**.

You simply type a backslash `\` followed by parentheses `()` inside your string, and place your variable inside the parentheses.

```swift
let score = 95
print("Your final score is \(score) points.")
```

quizData.question: Which of these uses correct String Interpolation to print a variable named 'age'?
quizData.explanation: Swift uses a backslash and parentheses `\(variable)` to inject values directly into a String.

slides.title: Math in Strings 🧮
slides.content:
# It works with Math, too!

String Interpolation is so powerful that you can put entire math expressions inside the parentheses.

```swift
let apples = 3
let oranges = 4
print("I have \(apples + oranges) fruits in total.")
// This will print: I have 7 fruits in total.
```

Swift calculates the math *first*, then places the result into your text!

quizData.question:
What will the following code output? 
print("5 times 2 is \(5 * 2)")
quizData.explanation: Swift will evaluate `5 * 2`, get `10`, and insert it into the resulting string: "5 times 2 is 10".

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Run this code and modify the variables to see how the interpolated string updates dynamically!
slides.codeSnippet:
let hero = "Batman"
let city = "Gotham"

print("\(hero) is from the city of \(city).")
